name: Prosper Center - South
description_short: Foundation Communities' Prosper Center – South offers free financial counseling, tax preparation, health insurance navigation, and college planning to low-income Austin residents.
description_long:
Foundation Communities is a housing-focused nonprofit serving Austin and North Texas that operates Prosper Centers—community resource hubs designed to expand access and opportunities for financial stability. Prosper Center – South, opened in 2019 with support from University Federal Credit Union, is located in South Central Austin near IH-35 and Oltorf Street, making it accessible via Cap Metro Bus Route 300 with free parking and wheelchair accessibility.

The center provides four core programs, all completely free: Health Coverage navigation (marketplace plan enrollment and insurance education regardless of income), Tax Help (IRS-certified tax preparation, amendments, prior-year returns, and ITIN applications), Financial Wellness coaching (credit improvement, debt payoff, budgeting, savings building, and YourTexasBenefits enrollment), and the College Hub (financial aid applications, scholarship assistance, college enrollment, and career planning). All services are available to individuals earning under $60,000/year and households of 2-4 earning under $85,000/year, with income limits increasing $5,000 per additional family member.

Prosper Center – South distinguishes itself as the newest location in the Foundation Communities network, purpose-built after 2019 to serve South Austin's diverse population. The center combines multiple financial assistance programs under one roof, eliminating the need to visit multiple agencies. Their IRS-certified tax preparers and trained financial coaches provide expert guidance, while health coverage navigation extends beyond income restrictions to serve all Texas residents regardless of earnings.

The primary limitation is that services are geographically restricted to the South Austin location and available only during specific hours that vary by program and season. While the income thresholds cover low-to-moderate-income households, the health coverage program's unrestricted availability may create capacity constraints. The organization's connection to housing development means their primary mission extends beyond financial services, though all Prosper Center programs are genuinely free with no hidden fees or upsells.
address: 2900 S I-35 Frontage Rd, Austin, TX 78704

company_info.headquarters: 2900 S I-35 Frontage Rd, Austin, TX 78704

pros: All services completely free with no fees, income verification barriers, or upsells
IRS-certified tax preparers provide comprehensive tax help including prior-year returns and ITIN applications
Health Coverage navigation available regardless of income level to all Texas residents
Trained financial coaches offer one-on-one support plus group classes for credit, debt, budgeting, and savings
College Hub provides integrated financial aid, scholarship applications, and career planning support
Located in wheelchair-accessible facility with free parking and direct bus access (Cap Metro Route 300)
Newest Prosper Center location (opened 2019) with updated facilities and dedicated South Austin service area
cons: Services limited to South Austin location only—no remote or phone-based counseling mentioned
Program hours vary by service with no published schedule, requiring phone call to 737-717-4000 to confirm availability
Income eligibility caps at $60,000 for individuals and $85,000 for 2-4 person households, excluding some moderate-income families
Limited information about wait times, appointment availability, or how to access services (no online booking system mentioned)
As a nonprofit focused primarily on housing, financial services may be secondary to organizational mission
best_for: Low-income South Austin residents earning under $60,000/year seeking free tax preparation and financial coaching
Families and individuals struggling with credit, debt, or budgeting who need professional financial guidance
Prospective and current college students needing free help with financial aid and scholarship applications
Texas residents of any income level seeking free health insurance marketplace navigation and enrollment assistance
diagnosis: Prosper Center – South is best for low-income South Austin residents (under $60,000 individual income) needing free, expert financial counseling, tax preparation, or college planning. The main caveat is that services are geographically restricted to the South Austin location and hours vary by program, requiring advance contact to confirm availability and schedule appointments.
services: Free IRS-certified tax return preparation and filing
Tax amendments and prior-year return filing
Health Insurance Marketplace plan review and enrollment
Insurance understanding and healthcare access guidance
One-on-one financial coaching for credit improvement, debt payoff, budgeting, and savings
College enrollment and degree pathway exploration
Career planning support through College Hub
